summary: Lead-time bias may explain why asymptomatic patients identified by screening have longer survival times; this does not indicate better outcomes but instead represents an artifact due to earlier diagnostic dates. Early detection improves measurement of disease-free interval without altering underlying tumor aggressiveness.
